transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Connect switching is a thing.
Are you still doing weekly meetings with your clients or have you been moving to bi-weekly?
Depends on the account.
Some actually switched from bi-weekly to weekly.
So it depends really on how complex the customer is.
Are you trying to...

I feel like for some of them, just because I feel like the ones that feel like we've got a good runway, they're happy with the strategy, it's like a steady state, especially the ones that didn't have much content.
And so there's just not a ton of reporting yet either.
I just feel like I spend so much time preparing for meetings and then having meetings.
If there was a way to move some clients to more of a bi-weekly cadence, it feels like it would just leave more room to do actual work.
G you.
Thank Very
Yeah, mean, like, having a focused time is really something that I'm struggling with.
Yeah, me too.
So I don't know.
I feel like I'm kind of just surviving and trying to get up to speed on all the accounts before the holidays.
And then I figured January, maybe I'll be able to, like, do better figuring out, like, the cadence.
But I'm just curious what people are doing.
Yeah, I get it.
So anyways, Ambient.
I was like, surprise!
So I met with Mariana this morning.
So she kind of gave me just a bit of an update from her perspective.
She flagged, you know, like, the lack of content runway.
So I'm, like, generally up to sleep.
I reviewed their, you know, artifacts.
So I was more curious from you.
I know you've only had them for a week, so you also don't have a ton of context.
But I didn't know.
Did you get to meet?
Who was their Emmy before?
So I think Mariana was actually their Emmy, and Mara was the director, and I think Tiandra, if I'm not mistaken, was producing content.
No, sorry, Susetta.
Okay.
So Mariana was there.
So that's why Mariana has all the context.
Okay, that makes sense.
So, yeah, I had a meeting with them last week, and I would say that they are a pretty interesting account, because I met Mau, Mauricio, and Atul, and then at the end of the meeting, the CMO joined.
So, like, Alberto was pretty engaged.
Mauricio was, like, just doing something on his phone, you know, throughout the meeting.
So weird.
It was, and he was, like, not really listening.
Like, I didn't feel like he's actively listening.
He was just like, you know, he was not happy with the transition, like, you know, losing Mara.
I said that, you know, Mariana is going to produce and edit the content and he kind of didn't get it right.
So he was like, oh, that changes again.
And so he was like very sensitive to any like change.
So they are a little bit like, you know, like sensitive to these like things.
Yeah.
Funny that they then asked for another change.
Yeah, I mean, yeah, I feel like they're very like interesting as people.
And what I thought, also like Alberto, so their CMO, they wanted to see trend month over month when it comes to, you know, data and analytics.
So I just fixed the dashboards to monthly from weekly to show the trend.
But they just published like 25 pieces and the growth was pretty good considering it's just 25, but at the same time, it's a mix of like branded and non-branded.
So the guy was like very happy about the result, but then he got into non-branded clicks and there is just like one or two clicks as they are just at the beginning of the journey.
So he was like very disappointed by like, I feel like they don't understand exactly how SEO works.
So you have to be very patient, very slow, and probably like explain the rationale behind any chart instead of just like getting them excited.
Because like, this is what's usually customers were happy in the past, just present them like everything.
But like, you know, they were like super, super picky and requested a couple of changes.
I can actually show you what, like what changed.
So do you feel like they have a good sense of like SEO works in terms of the timeline?
Because it also seems like expecting to see results when they just recently came out of Sprint and we've only published like 20 pieces of content.
I don't think so.
They just don't understand.
Yeah, that's the issue, like the issue is that like they're super sensitive about the content.
mean, actually, like, about everything, I think, because, like, they're super picky about the, you know, platform issues and any, like, messaging issue.
And then, you know, strategy is kind of like, there are a couple of improvements that are made to the strategy, especially to the reporting as well.
But, like, what, you know, what they saw is, I think, like, in September, things started picking up.
So we can actually check it together.
Let's see, referrals, well, so, yeah, I think there was nothing in August.
Okay, there was, so, okay, let's do this.
So, okay, so this is what I, what I showed them, you know, like, as a totals and engagement sessions, which are fine.
But then, I would say that, like, monthly reporting is preferred.
And then, I showed them this query destroyer.
And destroyer.
distribution from Google search console, right?
The thing is like you're seeing different colors here than typical colors is that like there was for them a lot of noise.
Like, you know, like average query position first, then second to 10th, then 11th to 20, I think, and then et cetera, et cetera, and plus 41.
So they were like not very happy.
So I asked Vivek and Suleiman to just basically merge these further queries into one.
So that's why you can see here, you know, plus 10 is just this orange.
So he was like happier with the result.
Like I just messaged him on Slack yesterday.
And then, you know, like they really want to focus on this.
But the issue last week was that, you know, I did export the queries from the sub to show them like what exactly was a query.
And the average position.
And I felt like they kind of don't.
can't quite understand how Google Search Console works and what does it mean to have like an average on position one for a very specific query.
Are they trying to Google themselves?
I've had this problem with other clients where they're like, we're not showing up at position three.
You told us we were at position three.
Yeah, and I feel like Mauri, no, like Alberto was very disappointed with this and I just felt like he doesn't trust me.
In a way that, you know, like I'm explaining, hey, this is an average, on average, you're on the first position across all the devices, all the states and all the locations.
So it's a log.
So it's the most accurate data you can have.
You can have also SEMrush data, which is a prediction or like, you know, what you can see for a specific location.
So there are just two different things and these two measure.
So you have to be, you know, like very explicit about it.
But I just felt like he still is not like very happy about, you know, that he's not seeing these queries.
And he's like, how can I prove to my boss something that is not visible, right?
So he was like very, yeah, there was like a lot of mistrust.
I don't know if it was the case before with Mariana and Mara.
And I'm not sure like to what extent they were presenting them any data.
But like somehow, like when it comes to cohorts, for example, there is just, you know, growthx URLs and non-growthx URLs.

So the way that he was updating it in the past was not putting, like there are clusters, but at the same time, if I pull the, their Conan OS, you can clearly see that like, okay, there's the dark of clusters, like industry specific, specific things, competitive comparisons, and threat detection, but somehow they're not.
They were not updating Looker dashboard according to these clusters, so like I had a little wiggle room of like actually proving which clusters are working and which are not, right?
So, but actually they didn't seem to care a lot about it, but maybe it is something that you could like fix for them and show, hey, we have the biggest impact here, et cetera, right?
Like this will be like a good leverage, I guess.
This is what I would do at least.
What else?
think, yeah, like when it comes to their review process.

So I talked to you in the ticket in the director's board because as you can see, there are just two ideas and I feel like, okay, we are like probably in January, we're still fine just because you can take a look at the, you know, client approved, the 14 topics and then in production, we have six and then ready for client review.
There are quite many.
What I did yesterday was I tagged, like I tagged.
them as a reviewer, just because like this was something that was done in the past.

The process was that like, you know, I don't know how they were sending them the content that the articles, but they were like tagging themselves as a reviewers and then checking the articles, which was like very inefficient because like no one wants to really tag, like put the tag in them and then review the content.
Otherwise, so what I, what I did was like, I just jumped in and I tagged them.
And what my plan going forward was to basically let them know like, hey, Alberto reviewed this, this, and that, Mau reviewed this, this, and that.
Right.
So like we speed things up, especially the Alberto.
So the guy who was like, not sure about GSC, he wants results and he wants to show it to, to his boss bosses.
Like there are like just two, two guys, I think, um, in the company, you know, the, the founder and co-founder.
So what I would try to do is just like, not in the, like a brute force.
think because they're pretty sensitive, but just like suggest, you know, some improvements to the, to the processes because like they expect results, 25 posts published in three months.
That's not a lot.
It's like, you know, probably like around eight posts per, per, per month instead of just 20.
So, you know.
And is that because they're just slow to review?
So we just have a lot of ones stuck in review or what do you, did you get a sense of why?
Yeah.
just, oh yeah, okay.
Just these things, right?
Like, do they tend to be, are they style things or are they like actual like?
Product.
Product.
Okay, that's product.
Yeah, okay.
Yeah, like messaging.
This is what I pick, you know, like too strong, strong, right?
Because they want to be very like balanced.
like physical security.
So I think like it falls very closely to some regulations or like what you can do, what you cannot do, how you can speak about it.
So they're very sensitive about the prorogate.

And, but Mariana is very hands-on with them, so, and also, I don't know, like, if she shared it, but just in case, like, client approved, this is a thing that, actually, they are approving a brief, so this is like a, I don't know, this is called draft URL, but just don't be, like, misled, just because, like, in the draft URL, later on, you have, like, an art, okay, it's not started, so let's present something else, like this one, for example, right?
So, yeah, so the draft here, it is just a brief, and the article is actually a draft.
okay.
So, an article.
They, you know, again, like, something to potentially remove going forward, just because it's, like, creating so many blockers in the, you know, like,
At the beginning of the collaboration, I understand that they want to know a keyword, the title, and maybe core thesis, but I don't believe like that long core thesis is like easy to read for them.
So probably they're like postponing these things.
So probably like removing some kind of fences that are existing for some reasons now is something to continue doing.
But as I said, like just one thing at a time, probably there will be like a lot of discussion around like you can watch the previous call, like how I introduced the change.
And they were like, the reaction was like, no, we don't want any, you know, any change.
I will go watch that.
Yeah, because this seems like, I don't know, it's like, it's very hard when clients want velocity, but they also want like 20 different reviews and all these other things that are going to slow.
And they don't seem to kind of connect the two.
Yep.
Yeah.
A lot of control and.
Yeah.
Um.
Yeah, this slows things, like a lot of collaboration, right?
So it's like slow.
Yeah.
Yeah, maybe.
Tricky a little from that, just from, it seems like we have a lot of trust to build to get them to where they want to be.
Yeah.
And this is what I did.
Maybe it was like four last calls as a summary.
So I did the summary for my new account.
So, you know, the context, product understanding, issues, production, workflow problems, et cetera.
So I was always like drawing from this list before the first call.
So maybe, maybe something helpful.
I can send it to you if you.
Yeah, that would be great.
I would love to take a look at that.
Okay.
And I think like I don't have more info.
Do you know why we got it handed with so few ideas, like topics?
Did it come out of Sprint with no runway or it just seems, yeah, that just seems like two, that's just not very many.
Yeah, think, like, probably they were not really, like, because, like, the process was so slow, no one plugged it.
And, yeah, like, I also understand Kyle's, you know, point of, like, we should be doing it in pods, but, like, not if, not if it's, like, just two topics.
So, like, we have a backlog for them, like, considering the, like, how slow the approval process is, but I just feel always comfortable having, like, 30, 40 topics approved in the backlog.
So we can, like, you know, maybe there was, like, you know, there will be, like, aha moment on your site that they will start trusting us more.
Probably you can round up the production to eight per week.
So there is also, like, a possibility of, you know, like, based on, like, what I was seeing in the contract, double check, but the renewal is not very, like, it's not, like, any Q1.
or Q2, so maybe like end of Q2, so I think they were pretty new, I got this out, hadn't been out of Sprint for very long.

Yeah, yeah, but also something that I was surprised with, like some of the customers that go out of Sprint, they got like six month contract, so it's not always annual, so sometimes they do flag, I was like, okay, we've done three months together, here are the results, they're okay, so let's see what happens in the next three months, so double check, you know, what are their expectations, because like it looked like CMO wants to present some data to the exec team, and he didn't have lever because of GSC issue, and what else, the one non-branded click, right?
In the last three months, so, you know.
So he was like, oh, I'll just stop for now and want to share anything.
So they're also probably internally also very sensitive to what they're presenting to executive team.
That's my feeling about the organization as a whole.
Yeah, okay.
These are all very good flags.
Okay.
All right.
No, I'm curious, like, you know, text me after.
Yeah, I'll let you know.
Yeah, it will be like, you know, like I keep fingers crossed for building this relationship because it's like, I would consider this is like a pretty challenging account to work with.
But I think you can do it, especially like you're US-based.
So I think.
Oh, man.
Okay, well, I'll let you know.
I'll let you know how it goes tomorrow.
Cool.
Yeah.
Good luck.
And if you need anything, you just hit me up.
All right.
I will.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Handover briefing for the Ambient account.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=85.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**High-Touch Client:** Ambient is highly sensitive to change and requires constant reassurance, especially after losing their previous director (Mara).](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=179.0)
  - [**Data Misalignment:** The CMO (Alberto) distrusts Google Search Console (GSC) data, as the "average position" metric doesn't match his manual searches. This prevents him from reporting results to his execs.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=428.0)
  - [**Production Bottleneck:** A slow, multi-stage review process (brief → article) and a hyper-critical client have limited output to \~8 posts/month, creating a content runway of only 2 approved topics.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=570.0)
  - [**Trust-Building Priority:** The immediate goal is to build trust by educating the client on SEO fundamentals and streamlining the review workflow.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=428.0)

## Topics

### Client Context & Dynamics

  - [**Team:** CMO (Alberto), Mauricio, and Atul.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=147.0)
  - [**Key Dynamic:** Mauricio is resistant to change, while Alberto is engaged but highly skeptical of results.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=147.0)
  - [**Organizational Pressure:** Alberto needs to prove ROI to his execs (the founders) and is hesitant to report on early-stage metrics like a single non-branded click.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=970.0)

### Production Bottleneck

  - [**Low Output:** The team has published only 25 posts in 3 months (\~8/month).](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [**Root Cause:** A slow, multi-stage review process.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
      - [**Stage 1:** Client approves a detailed brief ("draft URL").](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
      - [**Stage 2:** Client reviews the full article draft.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [**Impact:** This workflow has created a content runway of only 2 approved topics, risking production halts.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=570.0)

### Data & Reporting Misalignment

  - [**Client Request:** Monthly trend data, not weekly snapshots.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=213.0)
  - [**GSC Misunderstanding:** Alberto distrusts GSC's "average position" metric, as it doesn't match his manual searches.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=428.0)
      - [**Rationale:** He needs to "prove to my boss something that is not visible."](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=473.0)
  - [**Dashboard Simplification:** The query distribution chart was simplified (e.g., merging positions 11-20 and 21-30 into a single "+10" bucket) to reduce "noise" for the client.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=356.0)
  - [**Cohort Reporting Gap:** The Looker dashboard lacks clusters (e.g., "industry specific," "threat detection"), making it impossible to prove impact for specific content themes.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=498.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Bailey:**](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=803.0)
      - [Watch the previous client call recording to understand their resistance to change.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=803.0)
      - [Use the account summary template (to be provided by Jo) to prepare for the next meeting.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=854.0)
      - [Prioritize building trust by educating the client on SEO fundamentals and streamlining the review process.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=428.0)
      - [Investigate the contract renewal date (Q1/Q2) to understand the timeline for proving value.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=947.0)
  - [**Jo:**](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=854.0)
      - [Send the account summary template to Bailey.](https://fathom.video/share/J2CeEkMGSTcybyVAFaU1tvz6zEEEm_qi?tab=summary&timestamp=854.0)

action_items.description: Watch Ambient prior call recording re: change intro
Email Bailey Ambient account summary/context doc
Text Jo post-call update re: Ambient
